_comment: OPT-IN hooks showcase. Launch with:  claude --settings .claude/settings.hooks-showcase.json  then ask the agent to run a bash command (e.g. 'run: echo hi'). Three PreToolUse handlers fire so you can see the three teachable handler TYPES side by side: command (deterministic shell), http (deterministic external service), prompt (model-in-the-loop). This file is real and loadable (strict JSON). It does NOT touch the default demo — only this launched session uses it.

PreToolUse._comment: TYPE: command  — DETERMINISTIC shell. Exit 0 + JSON on stdout. `systemMessage` prints a USER-VISIBLE line; `additionalContext` injects agent-only context. No block here — the ADVISORY path (contrast guard-shell.sh, which exit-2 BLOCKS). Inline, no script needed.

hooks.command: printf '%s' '{"systemMessage":"[command hook] Advisory: deterministic shell hook fired on this Bash call.","hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"PreToolUse","additionalContext":"[command hook] Advisory to the agent: prefer a dry run before data-mutating commands."}}'

PreToolUse._comment: TYPE: http  — DETERMINISTIC external. POSTs the PreToolUse payload to a URL; the 2xx JSON response IS the decision. Points at a public echo (postman-echo) so the round-trip fires with NO server to host: it proves the POST happens. In production this URL is your policy service, which would return a permissionDecision:deny to block. The echo has no decision fields, so it's a successful no-op. (Avoid httpbin.org here — it frequently 503s.) To make http visibly BLOCK on stage, point url at a static mock returning {"hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"PreToolUse","permissionDecision":"deny","permissionDecisionReason":"..."}} (e.g. a mocky.io URL).

hooks.statusMessage: Checking command against policy service (http hook)...

PreToolUse._comment: TYPE: prompt  — MODEL-IN-THE-LOOP. Sends the prompt (with $ARGUMENTS = the payload JSON) to a fast Claude model for a single-turn judgment. Flexible/nuanced but reintroduces model judgment — use for advisory checks, not hard guarantees. Self-contained; no script or server.

hooks.statusMessage: Asking a model to sanity-check the command (prompt hook)...
hooks.prompt: You are a package-governance gate. BLOCK any command that installs software globally or system-wide (e.g. 'npm install -g', 'pip install' outside a venv, 'brew install', 'apt/yum install', or a curl|sh installer) — these bypass our vetted-dependency process. Approve everything else. If blocking, deny the tool call with a clear one-line reason naming the policy. Payload: $ARGUMENTS
